novel_features.name: Price-cut detection
novel_features.description: Surfaces every synced listing whose asking price dropped between syncs, with old price, new price, percent cut, and days on market at the cut.
novel_features.rationale: LoopNet shows only the current price. Detecting a cut requires a price_history time series across syncs that only the local store has.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find motivated sellers and re-priced assets — a price cut is the single strongest deal-sentiment signal LoopNet itself never exposes.
novel_features.group: Change tracking only the local store enables

novel_features.name: Days-on-market
novel_features.description: Computes true days on market for every live listing from the date the CLI first saw it, and flags aged inventory past a threshold.
novel_features.rationale: LoopNet hides days-on-market entirely. The CLI synthesizes it from first_seen timestamps in listing_snapshots.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to tell a fresh comp from a stale one and to find listings that have languished — both invisible on LoopNet.
novel_features.group: Change tracking only the local store enables

novel_features.name: Market velocity
novel_features.description: Reports absorption for a submarket: new listings, delistings, median days on market, and net supply change per period.
novel_features.rationale: Requires new/delisted counts and median DOM aggregated across the full snapshot history — no single LoopNet page carries a time dimension.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to gauge whether a submarket is heating up or cooling — the supply-and-demand pulse for a market-intelligence brief.
novel_features.group: Change tracking only the local store enables

novel_features.name: Delisting detection
novel_features.description: Lists listings present in a prior sync but absent now — sold, withdrawn, or expired.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a presence diff of listing_snapshots between syncs; a stateless scraper cannot know what disappeared.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to track which assets cleared the market — a proxy for transaction velocity LoopNet never publishes.
novel_features.group: Change tracking only the local store enables

novel_features.name: Cap-rate and yield distribution
novel_features.description: Reports the cap-rate, NOI, and price-per-square-foot distribution (count, min, median, quartiles, max) for synced for-sale listings in a submarket, and flags listings whose cap rate falls outside the interquartile range.
novel_features.rationale: A statistical distribution across listings is a local aggregation; LoopNet exposes per-listing cap rate but never the submarket spread.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to benchmark a single asset's yield against its submarket and spot mispriced cap rates.

novel_features.name: Distress and motivation scan
novel_features.description: Flags listings carrying motivation signals: price-reduced and must-sell keyword hits in the description, Ten-X auction listings, and recent price cuts.
novel_features.rationale: Deterministic keyword match over the synced description/highlights corpus plus auction flags and price-history joins — needs the local store, not a live page.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find distressed and motivated-seller assets in one sweep instead of reading every listing's free text.

novel_features.name: Submarket digest
novel_features.description: Rolls a synced submarket into one report: live supply count, recent price cuts, median days on market, new and delisted counts, and distress hits.
novel_features.rationale: Joins listings, price_history, and listing_snapshots in the local store — no single LoopNet page carries supply, change, and distress together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for a one-command market-intelligence snapshot of a submarket instead of running five separate analysis commands.

novel_features.name: Pipeline feed export
novel_features.description: Exports the latest synced submarket as a run-stamped JSON or CSV file, with records mapped to the six CRE market-intelligence data categories.
novel_features.rationale: Shapes local store rows into a clean, time-stamped feed for an external pipeline's ingest path — a deliberate integration surface no scraper offers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to drop LoopNet data straight into a downstream CRE pipeline (e.g. a data/raw ingest folder) without glue code.

narrative.headline: LoopNet shows you today — this CLI remembers, building the price-cut, days-on-market, and supply trends LoopNet never exposes.
narrative.value_prop: A SQLite-backed, agent-native CLI for LoopNet, the largest US commercial real estate marketplace. Search inventory and pull full listing detail like any scraper — then sync a submarket on a schedule and the local store accumulates the time series LoopNet hides. price-cuts finds every drop, dom computes true days-on-market, velocity tracks absorption, distress surfaces motivated sellers. Built to feed CRE market-intelligence pipelines: every command emits clean JSON or CSV.
narrative.auth_narrative: LoopNet's data pages are protected by Akamai Bot Manager, which blocks plain HTTP clients. The CLI fetches them by replaying short-lived clearance cookies from a real browser session. Run 'loopnet-pp-cli auth refresh' to mint cookies — it briefly drives the browser-use tool — or 'auth set --cookies' to paste a Cookie header copied from your browser's DevTools. Cookies last a few hours; refresh when a fetch fails with a bot-challenge error. No API key is required.

quickstart.comment: Mint Akamai clearance cookies (briefly opens a browser). Required before any live fetch.
Live search — confirm cookies work and listings parse.
Pull the submarket into the local store (fetches are paced ~10s for Akamai).
Cap-rate and yield distribution, computed offline from the store.
After a later sync, see which listings dropped their asking price.

troubleshoots.symptom: Fetch fails: 'Akamai bot-challenge page' / clearance cookies expired
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'loopnet-pp-cli auth refresh' to mint fresh cookies (or 'auth set --cookies "..."' to paste them). Cookies last a few hours; check state with 'auth status'.

troubleshoots.symptom: price-cuts, dom, velocity, or delisted return nothing
troubleshoots.fix: These read the local store and need at least two syncs of the same submarket over time. Run sync again after a gap, then re-run the command.
Each LoopNet fetch is paced ~10 seconds to stay under Akamai's bot-velocity threshold — this is expected. Lower --limit and --pages for a faster partial sync.

troubleshoots.symptom: inventory or sync returns 0 results for a location
troubleshoots.fix: Use LoopNet's slug form: a city-state like worcester-ma, a state like ma, or a 5-digit zip. Type accepts office, industrial, retail, multifamily, land, hospitality, health-care.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or analyst needs commercial real estate inventory, pricing, or market-intelligence data from LoopNet in a structured, scriptable form. It is the right choice for building a CRE data pipeline, tracking a submarket over time, screening for distressed or mispriced assets, or assembling comp sets. It is not for residential rentals (use Apartments.com) and it cannot access CoStar's subscriber-only analytics.

recipes.title: Seed and screen a submarket
recipes.command: loopnet-pp-cli sync worcester-ma --type industrial --listing for-sale && loopnet-pp-cli caprate worcester-ma --type industrial --agent
recipes.explanation: Pull a submarket into the store, then read its cap-rate and yield distribution in one pass.

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose detail record for an agent
recipes.explanation: Detail records carry tens of fields; --select returns only the address, price, cap rate, assessment, and broker so an agent does not burn context on the full payload.

recipes.title: Find every price cut this month
recipes.explanation: After repeated syncs, list listings whose asking price dropped in the last 30 days — the core deal-sentiment signal.

recipes.title: Export a market for a downstream pipeline
recipes.explanation: Write the latest synced submarket as a run-stamped CSV ready for a CRE market-intelligence ingest folder.

recipes.title: Roll up a submarket in one command
recipes.explanation: Supply, recent price cuts, median days on market, new and delisted counts, and distress hits for a submarket in a single report.

trigger_phrases: search loopnet for commercial real estate
find industrial properties for sale in
what is the cap rate distribution in
